inkwell-navy.$description: Inkwell Navy — Primary text, nav text, icon strokes, hairline borders — the structural graphite of every screen. Used as filled button background for primary actions to project institutional weight rather than enthusiasm

slate-600.$description: Slate 600 — Secondary borders and supporting text where Inkwell Navy is too heavy — outlines on cards, tertiary headings, subdued dividers

abyss.$description: Abyss — Dark code-block and terminal surfaces — deeper than Inkwell Navy so syntax highlighting reads with high voltage against the near-black

graphite.$description: Graphite — Muted helper text, inactive nav links, secondary button outlines — recedes so the navy headlines and mint accents can carry the hierarchy

steel.$description: Steel — Tertiary text and disabled-state borders — the quietest navy in the scale, used where information is supplementary

fog.$description: Fog — Page canvas — the warm off-white that holds all content; section bands alternate with pure white to create quiet vertical rhythm

pure-white.$description: Pure White — Card surfaces, form inputs, nav background — the elevated layer that floats above Fog with minimal shadow

silver.$description: Silver — Hairline dividers, input borders at rest, table separators — the thread that separates without announcing itself

mist.$description: Mist — Card edges and subtle surface tints where a second neutral step is needed between white and Fog

pewter.$description: Pewter — Body-level borders, subtle rule lines, the quietest non-white neutral

voltage.$description: Voltage — Announcement bar, high-attention data highlights, occasional feature accent — the single chromatic punctuation that earns the eye's attention by being used sparingly. Never decorates, always signals

mint-signal.$description: Mint Signal — Green outline accent for tags, dividers, and focused UI edges

untitled-sans.$description: Primary interface and display typeface — custom geometric grotesque. Weight 400 runs the body and UI; weight 500 lifts subheadings; weight 700 anchors the wordmark. Negative letter-spacing (-0.04em at display sizes, -0.01em at body) tightens the grotesque into a dense, architectural feel — the closer the eye gets to a heading, the more the letters close ranks, producing a precise, financial-document quality.

input-mono.$description: Code, API identifiers, numerical data, and inline technical labels. Alternates and stylistic sets ss01/ss02/ss12 are active — these micro-detailed glyph variants give the monospace a humanist, designed quality rather than a default-terminal feel, reinforcing that the product is engineered with intention.

page-canvas.$description: Surface level 0: Warm off-white field that holds all content sections; provides the neutral backdrop against which white cards and the chartreuse announcement bar gain their contrast.

card-surface.$description: Surface level 1: Pure white for product cards, feature panels, nav, and inputs — the layer that floats above canvas with subtle tinted shadow.

code-surface.$description: Surface level 2: Deepest surface in the system, used exclusively for code blocks and terminal demos; syntax colors (mint, blue, chartreuse) pop against it.

announcement-surface.$description: Surface level 3: Single-purpose full-bleed strip for time-sensitive messaging; the only surface in the system with native chromatic energy.
